title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain Himachal Pradesh judicial officer’s plea against Collegium picks for HC judgeship
content:
• On June 22, 2026, the Supreme Court, through a Bench including Justices B.V. Nagarathna and Joymalya Bagchi, refused to entertain a petition filed by judicial officer Arvind Malhotra against the Collegium's picks for Himachal Pradesh High Court judges.
• The Supreme Court Collegium, headed by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant, had on June 2 recommended Chirag Bhanu Singh, Bhupesh Sharma, and Yogesh Jaswal for elevation as judges of the Himachal Pradesh High Court.
• The Supreme Court observed that recommendations for appointments to constitutional courts are based on the Collegium’s subjective assessment, are amenable to only limited judicial review (e.g., for lack of effective consultation or eligibility issues), and seniority alone does not confer a right to be recommended for elevation.

title: 75,487 traffic e-challan cases settled in two days under 50% fine concession scheme
content:
• A total of 75,487 pending traffic e-challan cases were settled across Karnataka.
• ₹2.24 crore was collected through various prescribed modes of payment during the two-day period.
• The settlements were made under the State government’s recently announced 50% fine concession scheme.
